name: Simplification

sourceRefs.label: 2025_q1 page 125 Q191 (style reference: multi-step Aptitude word problem)
tnpscguru.in - TNPSC Aptitude Simplification Questions & Answers
standard BODMAS/fraction/surd/algebraic-identity patterns, no direct past-paper hit needed for these ŌĆö they are the topic's foundational mechanics

researchNotes: Math topics don't decompose into discrete trivia facts the way General Studies does ŌĆö the same handful of techniques (BODMAS order, fraction-of-decimal, ratio scaling, simultaneous equations, algebraic-identity shortcuts) get re-tested every year with different numbers. So the reading material teaches each technique once with a fully worked example (8 techniques, all kept ŌĆö no real overlap to dedupe), and the mock test applies the *same* techniques to fresh numbers (12 problems) rather than reusing the reading numbers verbatim, so genuine application is tested, not memorized arithmetic. Two reading items and one mock item are anchored to real sourced past-paper numbers; the rest are standard authored drill problems for these same patterns ŌĆö flagged transparently via sourceTag rather than dressed up as past-paper hits they aren't.

reading.intro: Simplification is 1 of 10 named Aptitude sub-skills (15 questions total for the whole Aptitude unit). TNPSC's simplification questions are rarely 'plug into one formula' ŌĆö they combine BODMAS, fractions/decimals, ratios, and algebraic shortcuts. Each item below is a worked method, not just an answer ŌĆö the goal is to recognize which technique a given expression calls for.

qa.question: Method ŌĆö BODMAS with a nested bracket and an unknown: Solve for x: 57 ŌłÆ [42 ŌłÆ {37 ŌłÆ (20 ŌłÆ x)}] = 176 ├Ę (8 ├Ę 2)
qa.answer: Work from the innermost bracket outward, and simplify the RHS first: 176 ├Ę (8 ├Ę 2) = 176 ├Ę 4 = 44. Now the LHS: (20 ŌłÆ x) stays as is for now ŌåÆ {37 ŌłÆ (20 ŌłÆ x)} = 17 + x ŌåÆ [42 ŌłÆ (17 + x)] = 25 ŌłÆ x ŌåÆ 57 ŌłÆ (25 ŌłÆ x) = 32 + x. Set equal to 44: 32 + x = 44 ŌåÆ x = 12. (Real TNPSC-style problem; this exact expression is a recurring coaching-site reference question.)

qa.question: Method ŌĆö sum of two squares without brute-force squaring: Find 217 ├Ś 217 + 183 ├Ś 183.
qa.answer: Use a┬▓ + b┬▓ = (a+b)┬▓ ŌłÆ 2ab instead of squaring two 3-digit numbers by hand. Here a+b = 400 (round number ŌĆö that's the clue this trick applies) and ab = 217 ├Ś 183 = 39,711. So a┬▓+b┬▓ = 400┬▓ ŌłÆ 2(39,711) = 160,000 ŌłÆ 79,422 = 80,578. Recognizing 'the two numbers add to a round number' is the trigger for this shortcut.

qa.question: Method ŌĆö difference of squares shortcut: Simplify (197 ├Ś 197 ŌłÆ 103 ├Ś 103) ├Ę (197 ŌłÆ 103) without long multiplication.
qa.answer: a┬▓ ŌłÆ b┬▓ = (a+b)(aŌłÆb), so (a┬▓ŌłÆb┬▓)/(aŌłÆb) = (a+b) directly ŌĆö no multiplication needed at all. Here a=197, b=103, so the answer is simply 197+103 = 300. This 'difference of squares divided by the difference' shape is a favorite TNPSC trick question precisely because it looks like it needs heavy calculation but collapses to one addition.

qa.question: Method ŌĆö fraction-of-decimal addition: Simplify 7/5 of 58 + 3/8 of 139.2.
Method ŌĆö ratio substitution to simplify a compound ratio expression: If x : y = 3 : 4, find (4x + 5y) : (5x ŌłÆ 2y).
qa.answer: Don't solve for x and y separately ŌĆö substitute x=3k, y=4k (k is just a scaling factor that will cancel). (4x+5y) = 12k+20k = 32k. (5xŌłÆ2y) = 15kŌłÆ8k = 7k. Ratio = 32k : 7k = 32 : 7. The k always cancels in a ratio question ŌĆö that's the whole trick.

qa.question: Method ŌĆö same ratio-substitution trick, different ratio: If x : y = 3 : 5, find (5x + 3y) : (15x ŌłÆ 2y).
Method ŌĆö simultaneous equations feeding into a simplification target: If 3a + 4b = 22 and 8a ŌłÆ 5b = ŌłÆ4, find (a+b)┬▓.
qa.answer: Solve the pair normally: from 3a+4b=22 and 8aŌłÆ5b=ŌłÆ4, elimination gives a=2, b=4 (check: 3(2)+4(4)=6+16=22 Ō£ō; 8(2)ŌłÆ5(4)=16ŌłÆ20=ŌłÆ4 Ō£ō). The question doesn't ask for a and b individually ŌĆö it asks for (a+b)┬▓ = (2+4)┬▓ = 36. TNPSC often hides the real target ((a+b)┬▓, not a or b) at the end of a simultaneous-equation question ŌĆö always re-read what's actually being asked before solving for individual variables.

qa.question: Method ŌĆö mixed numbers with a nested fraction: Simplify 2┬Į + 3Ōģō ├Ę Ōģö.
qa.answer: BODMAS still applies to fractions ŌĆö division before addition. 3Ōģō ├Ę Ōģö = (10/3) ├Ę (2/3) = (10/3) ├Ś (3/2) = 5. Then 2┬Į + 5 = 7┬Į. The common mistake is adding 2┬Į+3Ōģō first ŌĆö division always binds tighter than addition, fractions or not.

questions.prompt: Solve for x: 70 ŌłÆ [45 ŌłÆ {33 ŌłÆ (15 ŌłÆ x)}] = 171 ├Ę (9 ├Ę 3)
questions.explanation: RHS: 171├Ę(9├Ę3)=171├Ę3=57. LHS inner-out: {33ŌłÆ(15ŌłÆx)}=33ŌłÆ15+x=18+x ŌåÆ [45ŌłÆ(18+x)]=27ŌłÆx ŌåÆ 70ŌłÆ(27ŌłÆx)=43+x. Set 43+x=57 ŌåÆ x=14.
Using a┬▓ŌłÆb┬▓ = (a+b)(aŌłÆb), dividing by (aŌłÆb) leaves just (a+b) = 425+375 = 800.

questions.prompt: If p : q = 5 : 6, find (3p + 2q) : (4p ŌłÆ q).
If m : n = 4 : 7, find (2m + 3n) : (5m ŌłÆ n).
If 5a + 2b = 16 and 3a ŌłÆ 4b = ŌłÆ6, find the value of (a+b)┬▓.
questions.explanation: Multiply the first equation by 2: 10a+4b=32. Add to the second (3aŌłÆ4b=ŌłÆ6): 13a=26 ŌåÆ a=2. Substitute back: 5(2)+2b=16 ŌåÆ b=3. (a+b)┬▓=(2+3)┬▓=25.

questions.prompt: A trader borrows Ōé╣16,000 at 12% p.a. simple interest and another Ōé╣12,000 at 18% p.a. simple interest. In how many years will the two amounts owed (principal + interest) become equal?
questions.explanation: This mirrors a real 2025 TNPSC Aptitude question style (same numbers, Simple Interest unit) ŌĆö included here as a reminder that 'Simplification' skills (careful fraction/ratio handling) carry directly into other Aptitude sub-topics like Simple Interest. Set 16000+16000(0.12)t = 12000+12000(0.18)t and solve for t to get 16Ōģö years.
This is the (a+b)┬▓ pattern in disguise: a┬▓+2ab+b┬▓ = (a+b)┬▓. Here a=89, b=11, so the answer is (89+11)┬▓ = 100┬▓ = 10,000 ŌĆö no separate multiplication needed.
